Patch Set 6: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(1 inline comment)

I'm not exactly sure why, but this patch is repeatedly failing with crashes:

 BUG: unable to handle kernel NULL pointer dereference at 0000000000000290
 target_handle_connect+0x249f/0x2d00 [ptlrpc]
 mdt_connect+0x3a/0x4e0 [mdt]
 mdt_handle_common+0x648/0x1660 [mdt]
 mds_regular_handle+0x15/0x20 [mdt]
 ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x398/0xc60 [ptlrpc]
 cfs_timer_arm+0xe/0x10 [libcfs]
 lc_watchdog_touch+0x6f/0x170 [libcfs]
 ptlrpc_wait_event+0xa9/0x290 [ptlrpc]
 ptlrpc_main+0x0/0x1700 [ptlrpc]

If a patch is failing testing, then it always makes sense to investigate the cause of the failure.  If it is a single test hitting an intermittent failure, then a quick search in Maloo will show other similar failures and verifying the cause will be quick.  If a patch is consistently failing 7 different tests, it is time to look deeper.

As to _why_ this is failing, I can't say from looking at the patch, but I might guess it is in check_and_start_recovery_timer() causing the recovery service_timer not to be increased, and somehow a structure is being freed while it is in use.

Patch Set 6: Fails

Patch Set 6:

The changes with recovery timeout brings the issues with unprotected access to recovery timer. I propose to continue this patch set without this change and submit new LU ticket for discovered issue.

The crash happens in the following line:

lsi = s2lsi(obt->obt_sb);

because of obt->obt_sb is NULL. I need more investigation why this happens.
Uploaded patch set 7.

commit.subject: LU-3069 build: fix 'integer handling' issues
commit.message:
LU-3069 build: fix 'integer handling' issues

Fix 'program hangs' defects found by Coverity version 6.5.1:
Improper use of negative value (NEGATIVE_RETURNS)
Variable is passed to a parameter that cannot be negative.
Macro compares unsigned to 0 (NO_EFFECT)
This comparison is always true.
Operands don't affect result (CONSTANT_EXPRESSION_RESULT)
Test is always false regardless of the values of its operands.
Wrong operator used (CONSTANT_EXPRESSION_RESULT)
Test is always true regardless of the values of its operand.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I687a05aaffad13caaebe16edbc4f93ef7ca3413f
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/5888
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
